title: LangGraph: A Low-Level Orchestration Framework for Building and Deploying AI Agents
summary: LangGraph is an open-source framework designed to build, deploy, and manage complex generative AI agent workflows. It provides a set of tools and libraries that enable users to create, run, and optimize large language models in a scalable and efficient manner. LangGraph is a low-level orchestration framework that uses graph-based architectures to model and manage the relationships between various components of an AI agent.

sections.heading: Introduction to LangGraph
sections.content: LangGraph is an open-source framework created by LangChain, designed to build, deploy, and manage complex generative AI agent workflows. It is built on top of Python 3.10 or higher and is available under the MIT license. Installation is via pip: `pip install -U langgraph`. LangGraph is trusted by companies such as Klarna, Uber, J.P. Morgan, and more.

sections.heading: Key Features of LangGraph
sections.content: LangGraph is a low-level orchestration framework that provides a set of tools and libraries for building, managing, and deploying long-running, stateful agents. It uses graph-based architectures to model and manage the relationships between various components of an AI agent. LangGraph is inspired by Pregel and Apache Beam, and its public interface draws inspiration from NetworkX.

sections.heading: Comparison to Other Agent Frameworks
sections.content: LangGraph is different from other agent frameworks in that it provides a more expressive framework to handle companies' unique tasks without restricting users to a single black-box cognitive architecture. This makes it suitable for complex tasks that are bespoke to a company's needs.

key_takeaways: LangGraph is an open-source framework for building, deploying, and managing complex generative AI agent workflows
It provides a low-level orchestration framework for building, managing, and deploying long-running, stateful agents
LangGraph uses graph-based architectures to model and manage the relationships between various components of an AI agent
It is trusted by companies such as Klarna, Uber, J.P. Morgan, and more
LangGraph provides a more expressive framework to handle companies' unique tasks without restricting users to a single black-box cognitive architecture
